specs.Installation:
• Install the sensor parallel to the soil surface (horizontal) in the target layer. Bury at desired measurement depth and backfill gently.
• It should be surrounded with finer contents of aggregates.
specs.Note: The calulating equation of VMC for base course can use the calibrated equation while the one for subgrade remains unchanged.

specs.Installation:
• It can be directly placed and buried in soil following the layout. 
• The horizontal layout refers to "Instrumentation-DFOS".
specs.Note:
Temperature compensation is needed if value is less than 100. 
The issue of "ingredient" should be noticed and usage should collabrate with others.**

specs.Installation: • It can be directly placed and buried in soil.
specs.Note:
Water may leak through inside of the wire during F-T cycles so that the data logger connected needs to be higher than the sensor loacation. 
The wiring end need to touch each other to get the reading.

specs.Installation:
• LVDTs will be magnetically attached to a horizontal steel beam fixed om the frame to ensure vertical alignment. 
• The end touches the top of the cooling plate during F-T cycle and soil surface during plate loading.
specs.Note:
Expected displacement should not exceed its travel range.
The holder is custom 3D-printed.

specs.Installation:
• Horizontally place the pressure cell. 
• Ensure the entire sensing surface is fully in contact with the soil to avoid voids.
specs.Note: Perform calibration with known pressure loads (if applicable) before or after embedding

specs.Installation: • The actuator is installed on the top beam of the loading frame and will swing back during the compaction process to make room.
specs.Note: The loading frequency depends on the displacement needed. It is to simulate traffic loads.

specs.Installation:
• It will be placed on the top surface of base course and directly under the dynamic actuator. 
• 1/2" Thickness SBR sheet at bottom
specs.Note: The design is referenced from Dr. Pokharel's dissertation. It consists of 1" thick steel plate and about 10 mm-thick rubber base attached at the bottom to simulate rubber tire contact.

specs.Installation: • Wired with thin wires, and protect the finishing with epoxy.
specs.Note: Linearity error is <±8.6% (from 0 to 50% load), and repeatability is very good (<±2.5% of full scale after conditioning)

specs.Installation:
• The mounting surface must be prepared by cleaning and lightly abrading, then degreasing. 
• Pro-poxy 300 will be used as adhesive and protective coating.
specs.Note: The gauge should be bonded carefully to avoid any air bubbles and aligned to the principal strain direction. Temperature compensation is needed.

specs.Model: SF-20 Dual-Mass Hammer Dynamic Cone Penetrometer
specs.Installation: • Raise the sliding hammer to its top stop and let it free-fall onto the anvil; count every blow. After each blow  read and log cumulative penetration.
specs.Note: Choose the right rod length before performing tests
